purpose: The master agent's primary reference / hub. Defines the absolute non-negotiable constraints (master never writes code, all implementation dispatched to workers, dashboard mandatory, full-tracking thresholds at 3+ agents or >1 wave, master writes progress in lightweight mode, long prompts mean MORE planning not direct execution, read the command file every time). Provides a Module Index pointing at the nine files in agent/master/* (role, dashboard_writes, ui_map, eager_dispatch, failure_recovery, worker_prompts, compaction_recovery, dashboard_protocol, pki_integration), a high-level overview of how the dashboard works, eager dispatch summary, failure recovery summary with circuit breaker thresholds, worker-prompt construction summary, PKI integration summary, dashboard-write summary, role-and-constraints summary, compaction-recovery summary, the tracker layout tree, and a quick common-mistakes table. Master agents read this FIRST when invoking !p_track or any swarm command.
gotchas: ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINT 1: The master agent NEVER writes code — not a single line, not a 'quick fix', not even one helper. Every implementation is dispatched to a worker via the Task tool. Violating this defeats the entire purpose of Synapse.
Long or complex user prompts mean MORE decomposition and dispatching — never an excuse for the master to do the work directly.
PATH SAFETY: Always resolve {tracker_root} to absolute. Relative `dashboards/` paths from project_root CWD will write into the target project; a PreToolUse hook blocks misplaced writes.
Full-tracking thresholds (3+ agents OR >1 wave) are NON-NEGOTIABLE for multi-wave swarms — only `!p` lightweight mode is exempt.
In lightweight mode, the master MUST create minimal progress files from worker returns (files_changed, summary, completion logs) — otherwise dashboard shows no per-task file changes.
initialization.json is write-once. Only repair-task append and circuit-breaker replanning are exceptions.
Eager dispatch is on every worker completion, scanning ALL remaining tasks across ALL waves — never wait for a wave to finish, never batch by wave number.
Failed tasks do NOT satisfy dependencies — repair tasks (with `r`-suffixed IDs) replace them in the dependency chain.
Circuit breaker triggers automatic replanning when 3+ tasks fail in a wave, a single failure blocks 3+ downstream tasks, or a single failure blocks majority of remaining tasks.
Master may resume normal behavior (writing code) ONLY after the swarm completes — not 'almost done'.
patterns: Hub-and-modules layout: this file gives constraints + summary + module index, agent/master/*.md contain detailed procedures.
Five master responsibilities: Gather Context, Plan, Dispatch, Status, Report — nothing else.
Convention map → per-task filtered injection — master extracts CLAUDE.md categories from {project_root}, includes only relevant ones in each worker prompt.
Worker-prompt budget target ~8000 tokens (~800 lines), task duration target 1-5 minutes.

Master writes exactly four dashboard files: initialization.json (write-once static plan), logs.json (append-only event log), master_state.json (state checkpoint after every event), metrics.json (once at swarm end).
All dashboard writes are atomic — read full file, modify in memory, write full file.
PKI integration as enhancement-not-requirement — if no manifest.json exists, master proceeds with standard planning.
conventions: Top-of-file blockquote section for ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — bolded, numbered, with explicit STOP fallback line.
Module Index table with When-to-Read and per-row Constraint reminder columns.
Tracker layout shown as ASCII directory tree with comment annotations (e.g., `← Reserved (IDE agent...)`).
Quick Common Mistakes table at the end as a final scannable summary.
